key_insights.insight: Finding 'alpha' in entrepreneurship involves pursuing unobvious, complementary skills that create a unique advantage.
key_insights.quote: Phil had this idea that actually to sharpen your point, you should do something that is not obvious.

key_insights.insight: Skill stacking, especially with unobvious skills, can lead to a disproportionate advantage in a specific field.
key_insights.quote: I think it's a mathematical equation where if you're the best at six skills in an industry that are like unobvious, you actually are the best in a million people at that thing.

key_insights.insight: Learning from hobbies and activities outside your core business area can provide valuable insights and perspectives.
key_insights.quote: Both those things have made me, as a leader at Shopify, so much more valuable. I understand pretty much every feature and functionality that Shopify has because I tried for myself.

key_insights.insight: Building a connection with someone before a negotiation or business interaction can significantly improve the outcome.
key_insights.quote: If the pre-meeting went really well with the clients, no matter how the party went, it was going to be a great result.

key_insights.insight: Reading the room and adjusting your approach based on the audience's reaction is crucial for success.
key_insights.quote: One of the things about DJing you have to do is you have to read the audience.

key_insights.insight: Starting a business in school provides the perfect environment to begin with low expectations.
key_insights.quote: The reason I think is because when you're in school as a student,there's very few expectations on you that this is going to be a huge success.

actionable_advice.advice: Identify and develop 'spiky' skills outside your primary area of expertise.
actionable_advice.how_to: Consider skills in fields like theater, music (DJing), public speaking, storytelling or even philosophy. Dedicate time to learning and practicing these skills alongside your primary work.

actionable_advice.advice: Remove the expectation of immediate or huge success when starting a business, allowing for more experimentation and learning.
actionable_advice.how_to: Focus on the process of building and learning, rather than solely on the outcome. Set small, achievable goals and celebrate incremental progress.

actionable_advice.advice: When starting a business, look for partners with complementary skill sets rather than those who are similar to you.
actionable_advice.how_to: Actively seek out individuals from different fields or departments (e.g., arts, philosophy, engineering) who possess skills that complement your own.

memorable_stories: The story of Philip Reimer advising the guest to attend law school to become a better entrepreneur.
The story of the guest's experience as a DJ and how those lessons apply to business.
The story of his wife starting an ice cream business and his own hobby of starting a tea business and how both these things made him a better leader at Shopify
contrarian_claims: Going to law school can be more beneficial for entrepreneurs than business school.
Starting a company with friends from your own field is a bad idea.
Hobbies are equally important as focusing on core business area.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 130: Shopify's President: The 1 Skill You MUST Have To Thrive In Business: Harley Finkelstein
